text: A two-centimeter vertical incision is made through the umbilicus. The surgeon seals and cuts the bilateral round ligaments using a five-millimeter unseal device. The uterovesicle fold of peritoneum is lifted and incised. The bilateral ligamentum proprium and tuba uterinas are sealed and cut.
The surgeon achieves hemostasis to control back bleeding and uses a sponge to push the bladder downward, creating space for further dissection.
The surgeon seals and cuts the lateral uterine arteries. The cervical-vaginal junction is incised in a circular manner using a monopolar hook and end seal, assisted by the uterine manipulator.
Following the hysterectomy, the surgeon performs bilateral salpingectomy, removing the fallopian tubes. The vaginal cuff is sutured intracorporeally, completing the closure of the surgical site.
